title: Paspalanthium stoloniferum
intro: Paspalanthium stoloniferum, also known as 'Passion Flower Vine', is a perennial plant species belonging to the genus Paspalanthium, native to the Americas and characterized by its delicate, white flowers and long, vining stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and seizures, and is also used to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. The bioactive constituents of P. stoloniferum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are believed to be responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, P. stoloniferum is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its sedative and anti-anxiety properties.
taxonomy: Paspalanthium stoloniferum is classified within the family Eriocaulaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Paspalanthium, which is a small group of plants found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The family Eriocaulaceae is a group of monocotyledonous flowering plants commonly known as pipegrasses, which are found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. The classification of Paspalanthium stoloniferum is based on its unique morphological characteristics and molecular studies. Paspalanthium stoloniferum is a species of flowering plant that is native to South America.
